name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description: GenAI Client is a Java library for provider-neutral integration with generative AI and embedding services in Machai applications. It centralizes runtime provider resolution, model selection, prompt and instruction collection, file or URL input handling, function-tool registration, request execution, embedding generation, cleanup, and token-usage reporting. The library exposes a stable application-facing Genai contract while insulating callers from vendor-specific SDKs, authentication details, endpoint formats, and execution models. Supported integration areas include OpenAI-compatible chat and embedding services, Anthropic Claude message APIs, EPAM CodeMie delegated providers, and host-defined Java tools discovered through ServiceLoader. Typical use cases include semantic search, similarity comparison, automated content generation, repository summarization, AI-assisted project assembly, tool-calling workflows, controlled local capability exposure, and cross-request usage monitoring.

usageContext: Java applications that need a request-scoped abstraction over multiple GenAI providers
Machai modules that need to resolve providers from model identifiers such as OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini or Claude:claude-3-5-sonnet
Applications that need embeddings for semantic search or similarity comparison
Applications that expose curated host capabilities as AI-callable Java tools
Workflows that need token usage collection and reporting across multiple GenAI requests

constructors.signature: public static org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai getProvider(java.lang.String chatModel, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator conf)
constructors.description: Factory-style entry point for creating and initializing a request-scoped Genai provider from a provider/model string. The provider name and model are parsed from values such as OpenAI:gpt-4 or Claude:claude-3-5-sonnet. Provider names with dots are treated as fully qualified class names; otherwise the manager resolves the conventional provider class under org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.{provider}.{Provider}Provider. The selected provider is instantiated and initialized with the supplied Configurator.

examples.description: Creates an OpenAI-backed Genai provider for the gpt-4o-mini model using the runtime configuration object. Replace the null Configurator with the application configuration that supplies credentials, endpoints, and limits.

constructors.description: Factory-style entry point for creating and initializing an EmbeddingProvider from a provider/model string. It uses the same provider naming convention as getProvider and validates that the resolved class implements EmbeddingProvider. OpenAIProvider and CodeMieProvider are documented embedding-capable implementations.

examples.description: Creates an embedding provider for an OpenAI embedding model. The resulting provider can compute vectors for semantic search, similarity, or retrieval workflows.

constructors.description: Constructs a FunctionToolsLoader and discovers FunctionTools implementations using java.util.ServiceLoader. Discovered installers can then register compatible tool functions and prompt methods with a Genai provider.

examples.description: Creates the loader that scans the classpath for service-provided tool installers declared through the Java ServiceLoader mechanism.

features.name: Initialize provider
features.description: Initializes a concrete Genai provider with the selected model and runtime configuration. Configuration commonly supplies credentials, base URLs, request timeouts, output-token limits, tool-call limits, embedding model names, and input-log destinations.

examples.description: Directly initializes an OpenAI provider instance. In most applications, prefer org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getProvider so provider class resolution and initialization are handled together.

features.name: Add system instructions
features.description: Sets system or session instructions for the current conversation before prompts are executed. Instructions guide the provider behavior for the accumulated request state.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai provider = org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getProvider("OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini", conf);
provider.instructions("You are a concise technical assistant.");
examples.description: Adds a system instruction that shapes the response style for subsequent prompts in the same request-scoped provider instance.

features.name: Add prompt
features.description: Adds a user prompt to the current provider session. Providers may accumulate multiple prompts and other context before perform is invoked.

examples.snippet: provider.prompt("Summarize this repository and list the main integration points.");
examples.description: Queues a user prompt for execution by the initialized provider.

features.name: Execute request
features.description: Executes the provider request using accumulated instructions, prompts, files, registered tools, and provider state, then returns the generated response as a string. Providers may support vendor-specific behavior such as OpenAI Responses API calls, Anthropic message requests, or CodeMie delegated endpoints.

examples.description: Runs a simple prompt execution workflow and stores the generated response.

features.name: Clear provider state
features.description: Clears stored files, accumulated prompts, conversation state, and provider-specific session state. Provider instances are mutable and should generally be treated as request-scoped objects; clear can be used when reusing an instance for a new conversation.

examples.description: Resets the provider after a request so subsequent interactions start without previous context.

features.name: Compute embedding
features.description: Computes an embedding vector for input text using an initialized embedding-capable provider. Embeddings are useful for semantic search, similarity comparison, retrieval, and other natural language processing workflows. The requested dimensionality is provider-specific.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider embeddings = org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getEmbeddingProvider("OpenAI:text-embedding-3-small", conf);
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vector = embeddings.embedding("example text", 384L);
examples.description: Generates a numeric vector for text that can be stored in a vector index or compared against other embeddings.

features.name: Apply service-loaded function tools
features.description: Applies discovered FunctionTools installers to a Genai provider, filtered by application class compatibility. Compatible tools register both tool functions and prompt methods with the provider. This is the primary hook for exposing curated host capabilities such as file access, command execution, HTTP retrieval, or domain-specific operations to providers that support tool execution.

examples.description: Discovers FunctionTools implementations from the classpath and registers only those compatible with the application class before the provider is executed.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
customizations.description: Service-provider interface for installing host-provided function tools. Implementations are discovered through java.util.ServiceLoader and can contribute methods annotated as tools or prompts to compatible Genai providers.

examples.snippet: // Provide an implementation of org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools and list it in META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools so org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader can discover it.
examples.description: Shows the ServiceLoader-based customization model for adding application-specific AI-callable capabilities.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Tool
customizations.description: Annotation used to mark a Java method as a tool function within the AI provider framework so it can be registered and invoked during a run by providers that support tool calling.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Prompt
customizations.description: Annotation used to mark a Java method as a prompt provider within the AI provider framework so FunctionToolsLoader can register prompt contributions for a provider run.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Param
customizations.description: Annotation used to mark a method parameter as a Tool or Prompt parameter and provide metadata for AI provider tool definitions.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai
studs.description: Primary contract for a generative-AI provider integration. Implementations collect prompts and system instructions, attach local or remote files, register tool functions, execute requests, reset state, and expose request-scoped behavior for providers such as OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, CodeMie, or custom provider classes.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider
studs.description: Contract for providers that generate embedding vectors from input text. Implementations initialize with a model and Configurator and compute java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vectors for semantic search and similarity workflows.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction
studs.description: Functional interface representing a tool callable by a provider during a run. It is part of the structured invocation infrastructure for host-defined function tools.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
studs.description: SPI interface for installing host-provided function tools that are discovered by FunctionToolsLoader and registered with a Genai provider.

examples.title: Install the library in a Maven project
steps: Add the Maven dependency for org.machanism.machai:genai-client:1.2.0-SNAPSHOT to the application pom.xml.
Ensure the application also provides runtime configuration for the selected provider, for example OpenAI credentials for OpenAI models or Anthropic credentials for Claude models.
Use GenaiProviderManager to resolve a provider from a provider/model string.
examples.description: Dependency configuration for consuming GenAI Client from Maven-compatible builds.

examples.title: Run a chat completion workflow
examples.configuration: Configure provider credentials and endpoints in the application Configurator. OpenAI deployments typically use OPENAI_API_KEY and optionally OPENAI_BASE_URL. Anthropic deployments typically use ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and optionally ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL. CodeMie deployments use credentials such as GENAI_USERNAME, GENAI_PASSWORD, and an optional AUTH_URL before delegating to a downstream endpoint.
examples.description: Practical request-scoped usage pattern: resolve the provider, initialize it through the manager, add instructions and a prompt, execute the request, consume the answer, and clear state.

examples.title: Generate embeddings for semantic search
examples.configuration: Configure the embedding-capable provider with credentials and any provider-specific embedding model settings. OpenAIProvider and CodeMieProvider are documented embedding implementations.
examples.description: Creates an embedding vector suitable for storage in a vector index or for similarity comparison against other text vectors.

examples.title: Enable host-defined function tools
steps: Implement org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools in the application or a supporting library.
Annotate tool methods with org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Tool and prompt methods with org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Prompt; use org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Param for parameter metadata.
Register the FunctionTools implementation through Java ServiceLoader metadata under META-INF/services.
Create a FunctionToolsLoader and call applyTools before provider.perform().
examples.description: Demonstrates how to expose curated application capabilities to a provider that supports tool execution. Tool implementations should be configured and restricted deliberately because they may access host resources or remote endpoints.
